briefs.title: Tobacco and Vapour Retailers Must Display New Warning Decals by April 1, 2026
briefs.summary: Starting April 1, 2026, BC dealers who sell tobacco or vapour products must display two new warning decals: one facing customers at the point of sale, and a separate one visible to staff at the cash register. The old decals (Schedules 1 to 1.3) are being replaced. Vending machine operators must attach the customer-facing decal to the front of the machine but do not need the staff-facing one.
briefs.action_required: Replace your current tobacco/vapour warning decals with the new Schedule 1.4 (customer) and 1.5 (staff) decals before April 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC tobacco/vapour new decal display rules take effect
